sections.paragraph:
συγχρωτίζομαι (cp. the adv. συγχρῶτα, ‘body to body’; Hecato on Zeno the Stoic in Diog. L. 7, 2; Herm. Wr. 10, 17) pf. ptc. gen. συγκεχρωτισμένου (Ath. 24, 2) to be in defiling contact with, defile by touching τινός Dg 12:8.—DELG s.v. χρώς.

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Zeno Zeno , the Stoic, IV–III b.c.—List 5

Stoic Stoic , various dates—List 5

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

  1. pf. = perfect

ptc. ptc. = participle

gen. gen. = genitive

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 954
